data.title: SoundHound AI Stock Gains Over 6%, Let’s Look at Who Owns SOUN
data.description:
Voice AI software company SoundHound AI (SOUN) stock is up over 6% in the pre-market trading after the company reported strong Q4 earnings. EPS came in at -$0.05, beating analysts’ estimate of -$0.10. Meanwhile, revenue rose 101% year-over-year to $34.5 million. CEO Keyvan Mohajer credited the growth to major customer wins and expanding partnerships, including deals with Burger King UK and Lucid Motors (LCID). Given these developments, itâs the right time to delve into the ownership structure of SOUN.

Now, according toÂ TipRanksâ ownership page, public companies and individual investors own 69.83% of MU. They are followed by ETFs, mutual funds, other institutional investors, and insiders at 15.11%, 8.85%, and 4.07%, and 2.14%, respectively.

Digging Deeper into SOUNâs Ownership Structure

Looking closely at top shareholders, Vanguard owns the highest stake in SOUN at 8.41%. Next up is Vanguard Index Funds, which holds a 3.99% stake in the company.

Among the top ETF holders, Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI) owns a 2.79% stake in Boeing stock, followed by iShares Russell 2000 ETF (IWM) with a 2.30% stake.

Moving to Mutual Funds, Vanguard Index Funds holds about 3.99% of SOUN, whereas Vanguard World Fund owns 1.77% of the stock.

Is SoundHound Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SOUN stock based on 2 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. TheÂ average SOUN price target of $16.50 per shareÂ implies 79.15% upside potential.

See more SOUN analyst ratings

Conclusion

data.title: Barclays Hikes the Price Target on JD.com Stock Ahead of Q4 Results
data.description:
JD.com (JD)Â stock forecast received a boost from Barclaysâ price target hike ahead of its Q4 results. AnalystÂ Gregory Zhao of BarclaysÂ lifted the price target on JD stock to $55 from $50 yesterday, while maintaining his âBuyâ rating. The revised price target implies an impressive 29.3% upside potential from current levels.

Meanwhile, JD stock has surged more than 91% over the past year, driven by cost savings, improving consumer sentiment, and operational efficiency. Additionally, the company’s partnerships with other brands have contributed to its growth in the market.

Zhao’s Views on JD Stock

Zhao expects JD to deliver around 10% year-over-year revenue growth in Q4, supported by strong profit margins. He believes that the Chinese government’s economic stimulus measures will play a major role in driving growth through 2025, positioning JD to benefit significantly. With these positive factors in mind, Zhao increased his estimates, leading to the higher price target and reaffirming his optimistic outlook on the stock.

Bank of America Stays Bullish on JD With $48 PT

Similarly, Bank of America analyst Joyce Ju also maintained a “Buy” rating on JD.com with a $48 price target ahead of Q4 results. Ju expects strong Q4 2024 results, supported by government subsidies and promotions. She forecasts 10.1% year-over-year revenue growth, driven by trade-in programs and Singles Day sales.She believes ongoing government stimulus will continue to drive growth in JD’s core electronics and home appliances businesses through 2025.

Whatâs Next for JD Stock?

JD will report its Q4 results next week on March 6. The Q4 2024 results are likely to reflect the gains from the Chinese government’s stimulus, boosting sales in a few categories, including home appliances and electronics.

Wall Street analysts expect the company to report earnings of $0.85 per share, representing a 17% increase year-over-year. At the same time, revenues are expected to grow by 7% from the year-ago quarter to $45.4 billion, according to data from the TipRanks Forecast page. Notably, it has consistently beaten EPS estimates for the past nine quarters.

Is JD.com Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on JD stock based on eight Buys and one Hold recommendation assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. TheÂ average JD stock price targetÂ is $50.13, implying an upside potential of 17.84%.

data.title: SEC Backs Down as Coinbase Lawsuit Ends in Surprise Dismissal
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has officially dismissed its lawsuit against Coinbase (COIN). This shows us that huge changes are taking place in the SEC’s regulatory stance on crypto. According to court filings, the SEC filed a voluntary dismissal on Feb. 27, permanently ending all litigation tied to Coinbase and Coinbase Global. This includes dropping its initial June 2023 lawsuit and withdrawing its appeal with the U.S. Court of Appeals.

Crypto Regulation Changes

This move follows a broader pattern of the SEC retreating from legal battles against crypto firms. Over the past week, the agency has also dropped lawsuits against Consensys, Robinhood (HOOD), Gemini, Uniswap (UNI-USD), and OpenSea. According to SEC acting chair Mark Uyeda, the decision reflects a need to “rectify its approach” and create crypto policies through more transparent regulation, rather than legal action.

Coinbase Fought Back and Won

Coinbase had strongly pushed back against the lawsuit, arguing that the SEC was overreaching its authority. The case, filed in June 2023, alleged that Coinbase operated as an unregistered broker and offered securities such as Solana (SOL-USD), Cardano (ADA-USD), and Polygon (MATIC-USD) without proper disclosure. However, Coinbase maintained that the SECâs actions contradicted its approval for public listing on Nasdaq in 2021.

Is Coinbase Stock a Good Buy?

Analysts remain cautiously bullish about COIN stock, with a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 10 Buys and 12 Holds. Over the past year, COIN has increased byÂ 3.8%, and theÂ average COIN price targetÂ of $348.50 implies an upside potential of 67.3% from current levels.

See more COIN analyst ratings
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 2/28/25 – Futures Steady Ahead of Key Inflation Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were steady on Friday morning, as investors awaited the release of the key Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index report. This report, a key indicator of inflation closely monitored by the Federal Reserve, might influence market sentiment. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.28%, 0.19%, and 0.26%, respectively, at 3:42 a.m. EST, February 28.

In Thursday’s trading session, all major indices closed in the red. The Dow Jones and the S&P 500 both hit six-week lows and fell by 0.45% and 1.59%, respectively. Also, the Nasdaq Composite (NDAQ) suffered the most, declining 2.78% to a nearly four-month low.

Several factors impacted Investor sentiment, such as renewed trade concerns due to President Trump’s tariff proposals that raised concerns about the impact on global economic growth, and an 8.5% drop in Nvidia (NVDA) stock following its Q4 earnings report.

It is worth highlighting that todayâs trading session would mark the end of a challenging month. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite is down 5.5% so far this month. Further, the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones have also witnessed losses of nearly 3% for the month. Market volatility can be attributed to a lack of clarity regarding tariffs, persistent inflation, and weak consumer spending.

Friday’s trading session will be heavily influenced by the release of the January PCE price index data. Economists anticipate a 0.3% monthly increase and a 2.5% annual rise in the overall PCE, with core PCE expected to show similar growth.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year treasury yield was down, floating near 4.244%. Simultaneously, WTI crude oil futures are trending lower, hovering near $69.67 per barrel as of the last check.

Elsewhere, European indices opened lower on Friday on Trumpâs renewed threats to impose tariffs on the EU.

Asia-Pacific Markets Ended Lower on Friday

Asia-Pacific indices were in the red today after Trump confirmed that tariffs on imports from China, Mexico, and Canada will be imposed on March 4.

Notably, Hong Kongâs Hang Seng Index was down 3.28%. Further, Chinaâs Shanghai Composite and Shenzhen Component indices declined 1.98% and 2.89%, respectively. At the same time, Japanâs Nikkei and Topix indices closed lower by 2.88% and 1.98%, respectively.

Interested in more economic insights?â¯Tune in to our LIVE webinar.

data.title: Li Auto: This Chinese EV Maker Is Set for a “Comeback in 2025 and Beyond,” Says JPMorgan
data.description:
Li Auto (LI) stock forecast received a boost from JPMorganâs price target hike. Analyst Nick Lai of J.P. Morgan lifted the price target on LI stock to $40 from $22 today. The revised price target implies an impressive 25.3% upside potential from current levels. Further, Lia upgraded the stockâs rating to Overweight (equivalent to Buy) from Neutral.

Li Auto is a Chinese automobile manufacturer, offering a range of smart electric vehicles (EVs).

Laiâs Views on LI Stock

Lai believes Li Auto is set for a rebound in 2025 and beyond. The companyâs stock gained after revealing a photo of its second battery electric vehicle (BEV), the âi8,â on February 25. Though details on i8’s pricing and specs are limited, Lai sees it as a key step in Li Autoâs BEV strategy after the âMegaâ model failed in Q2 2024, causing a 42% share price drop.

Lai expects a soft launch of the i8 at the Shanghai Auto Show in April, with more BEVs like the i6, i7, and i9 to follow over the next 12 to 18 months. He predicts Li Autoâs sales could double to 1 million units by 2027, with over 35% coming from new BEV models.

Lai also notes that Li Auto is ahead of most Chinese rivals in L2+ semi-autonomous driving technology. Based on these factors, he upgraded the stock to Overweight and set a price target of $40.

Is Li Auto a Good Stock to Buy Today?Â 

On TipRanks,Â LI stockÂ has been assigned a Strong Buy rating, backed by three Buy and one Hold recommendations. TheÂ Li Auto share priceÂ target is $29.90, which is 6.33% below the current price level.

data.title: Meta (META) Ousts 20 Employees Over Internal Data Leaks to Media
data.description:
Facebook parent Meta Platforms (META) ousted 20 employees for leaking internal data to media outlets, despite consistent reminders to maintain a strict privacy policy. The news was first reported by The Verge, citing Meta spokesperson Dave Arnold. Meta said that it is still probing individuals for alleged data leaks and there could be more firings. CEO Mark Zuckerberg is miffed that employees are leaking confidential information shared during internal meetings and through memos.

Zuckerberg Is Upset with the Growing Internal Leaks

Meta is undertaking a rigorous stance against unauthorized disclosures by employees, regardless of the intent. A Meta spokesperson said that the company sends periodic reminders to employees that sharing internal confidential information is strictly against its policy. The company did not disclose what information was shared or to whom, and how it identified the leakers. Usually, the information relates to unannounced product launches and management comments on internal matters.

Whatâs funny is that even Zuckerbergâs frustrated comments about the internal leaks during a meeting with employees were shared with 404 Media. Zuckerberg says that while the company is trying to be as open as possible, employees are not valuing the transparency. Â 

Meta Undergoes Drastic Policy Changes

Metaâs stringent stance comes at a crucial time when the company as a whole is undergoing drastic policy changes. Meta has undertaken a mass layoff of roughly 3,000 workers claiming they were âunderperforming employees.â  The Instagram parent also changed its content moderation policy by ending the fact-checking model. Additionally, Meta recently ended its DEI (Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion) programs to align with the Trump administrationâs policies. Some say all these policy changes are aimed at finding favor with President Donald Trump, given the recent closeness between Zuckerberg and the President.

Is META Stock a Good Buy?

Wall Street remains highly optimistic about Meta Platforms stock prediction. On TipRanks, META stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 44 Buys, three Holds, and one Sell rating. The average Meta Platforms price target of $764.61 implies 16.2% upside potential from current levels. In the past year, META stockâs price has gained 36.4%.

See more META analyst ratings

Disclosure
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Sam Altman’s OpenAI Debuts GPT-4.5 and Calls It a “Giant Expensive Model”
data.description:
OpenAI just introduced GPT-4.5, calling it the most human-like AI yet. According to OpenAIâs CEO Sam Altman, the model delivers thoughtful replies and surprisingly good advice. It also picks up on subtle emotional cues, making conversations feel more natural, but it comes with a steep price.

Altman shared his excitement on X, writing, “GPT-4.5 is ready!” Despite calling it an expensive model, he described it as the first AI that feels like talking to a thoughtful person, even admitting heâs been “astonished” by how good the advice is.

Why GPT-4.5 Stands Out

The real game-changer is its emotional intelligence. GPT-4.5 can understand and respond to subtle human feelings more accurately. This reduces the chances of it making mistakes, also known as “hallucinations.”

In a blog post, OpenAI said GPT-4.5 has “broader knowledge and a deeper understanding of the world, leading to reduced hallucinations,” making it more reliable across different topics.

A Price Tag That Turns Heads

While GPT-4.5 brings smarter conversations, itâs not cheap. Altman didnât hide the price tag, calling it a “giant, expensive model.“ And heâs not exaggerating. The new model costs $75 per million input tokens and $150 per million output tokens, a massive jump from GPT-4âs $2.50.

Despite its premium pricing, its access is limited. For now, itâs only available to ChatGPT Pro users who spend $200 per month. Altman admits thereâs a shortage of GPUs, limiting its access. However, the company assured that efforts are underway to expand access to other subscription tiers soon.

GPT-4.5âs Release Heats Up the AI Race

OpenAIâs launch of GPT-4.5 comes as competition heats up, with rivals like Anthropic and xAI releasing advanced models. Anthropic recently introduced Claude 3.7 Sonnet, while Elon Muskâs xAI unveiled Grok-3 on February 13, claiming it has over 10 times the computing power of its predecessor.

With these competitors pushing forward, GPT-4.5 marks OpenAIâs latest effort to stay ahead in the AI race.

What Are the Best AI Stocks?

With AIâs role in society ever expanding, investors should monitor analyst opinions on AI stocks. Here at TipRanks, we have rounded up the best stocks that analysts are bullish about, using theÂ TipRanks Stock ComparisonÂ tool. From this graphic, it appears that analysts are most bullish about Nvidia (NVDA), Amazon (AMZN), Micron (MU), Meta (META), and Microsoft (MSFT).

Disclosure
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Nvidia’s (NVDA) Margins Are a Concern, Says Wall Street Analysts
data.description:
Nvidia Corporation (NVDA) stock recently experienced an 8.48% decline, a movement that has raised questions among investors. Despite the company’s impressive Q4 earnings numbers, the Wall Street analysts remain concerned over NVDAâs future profit margins.

Analysts Highlight Margin Pressures amidst Growth

Benchmark analyst Cody Acree said that that Nvidiaâs efforts to meet strong Blackwell chip demand have led the company to prioritize production over cost-cutting. This move may continue to pressure short-term gross margins. For the next quarter, Nvidia expects a 71% margin, down from 73.5% in Q4. However, looking ahead, Acree thinks that margins will either stay the same or rise slightly to about 71.0%-71.5% in Q2.

Also, DA Davidson analyst Gil Luria flagged margin concerns. The analyst expects fluctuations in NVDAâs profit margins associated with the rapid release of new GPU chips. Historically, new product launches have temporarily reduced gross margins. He noted that the anticipated 71% margin reflects these pressures and suggests future profitability could face further strain.

Luria also warned that chip oversupply risks could grow as major tech firms like Microsoft (MSFT), Meta (META), and Amazon (AMZN) plan to reduce their spending on advanced chips. This cut in spending could leave Nvidia with more chips than the market needs. At the same time, Chinese rivals are becoming stronger. Many labs in China are now using Huawei’s GPU chips for AI tasks instead of Nvidia’s, which could add to oversupply concerns.

Is NVDA a Good Stock to Buy?Â 

Overall, analysts remain bullish on NVDA stock, with a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 38 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months. AfterÂ a 55% rally in its share priceÂ over the past year, theÂ average NVDA price target of $178.66 per shareÂ implies an upside potential of 48.7% from current levels.

See more NVDA analyst ratings

Disclosureâ¯

data.title: Tempus AI (TEM) Shares Continue Slide Despite Ark Invests Buy-on-the-Dip Efforts
data.description:
A remarkable start to the year, marked by a 160% surge, has had investors paying close attention to healthcare tech company Tempus AI (TEM). Yet, over the past five days, the stock has lost roughly a quarter of its value, partly catalyzed by the company’s reporting Q4 results that fell short of revenue and earnings estimates. However, the Ark Innovation ETF (ARKK) and the Ark Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG), led by renowned investor Cathie Wood, have viewed this as a buying opportunity, buying over 400,000 Tempus AI shares. Yet, despite the vote of confidence, shares continue on a downward trajectory.

News Generating Positive Buzz

Tempus is a technology company that integrates artificial intelligence, notably generative AI, in healthcare for efficient diagnostics. The company holds one of the largest clinical and molecular data libraries globally. It offers an Intelligent Diagnostic system that continuously learns from its extensive data to inform physicians’ decisions in real time.  

The company recently acquired genetic testing pioneer Ambry Genetics in a strategic move to utilize its diagnostics and data to fuel innovation and enhance solutions for patients, clinicians, and life science companies.

On another positive note for Tempus, they recently announced a collaborative project with the Institute for Follicular Lymphoma Innovation. The collaboration aims to create a follicular lymphoma data library, which is expected to provide crucial insights into the disease.

The company has also grabbed the attention of major investors, with Ark Invest and Cathie Wood demonstrating their bullish stance by becoming the largest institutional shareholders. Wood’s faith in the company’s long-term promise is evident in her decision to invest over $1 billion across the ARK complex.

Top and Bottom-line Misses Despite Solid Growth

The fourth quarter of 2024 saw a 35.8% year-over-year increase in revenue of $200.7 million, yet this fell short of analyst expectations by $2.1 million. While the company posted a net loss of $13.0 million, this represented a significant decrease compared to Q4 2023 ($50.5 million loss) and Q3 2024 ($75.8 million loss). The non-GAAP earnings per share (EPS) of $0.18 missed consensus expectations by $0.03.

For the full year of 2024, annual revenue increased by 30.4% year-over-year to $693.4 million.  Although the company experienced a net loss of $705.8 million in 2024, it ended the year with a promising $940 million in remaining Total Contract Value. The company estimates an approximately 79% annual growth in 2025, with an expected revenue of around $1.24 billion.

Analysts Remain Cautiously Optimistic

Analysts following the company have expressed cautious optimism. For instance, in a post-earnings announcement, Needham’s Ryan MacDonald reiterated a Buy rating and increased the price target to $70.00 (from $56.00), noting the company’s recent solid performance and promising growth potential. He anticipates the acquisition of Ambry will significantly enhance Tempus’s growth, with the core business poised to grow approximately 30% and Ambry contributing to mid-to-high teens growth.

Tempus AI is rated a Moderate Buy overall, based on the recent recommendations of 10 analysts. The average price target for TEM stock is $66.22, which represents a potential upside of 19.75% from current levels.

See more TEM analyst ratings.

Disclosure
data.author: Casey Dylan, CIMA

data.title: Alcoa (AA) Weathering the Storm Amid Rising Specter of Aluminum Tariffs
data.description:
Alcoa Corporation (AA) has seen its shares slide as the global aluminum market faces considerable changes catalyzed by Trump’s 25% tariffs on all aluminum and steel imports. Reports suggest that this could prompt a reshuffling of global trade patterns and an increase in costs for US buyers. Alcoa’s strategic actions, including acquiring Alumina Limited, have positioned it as one of the world’s largest bauxite and alumina producers. The company has seen growth over the past year, primarily driven by the high demand for aluminum in Europe and North America, coupled with strong performance in the electrical and packaging markets and a recovery in the building and construction sector. While it’s likely tariffs will have little impact on the company’s margins as cost increases will be passed through to end buyers, it’s not all smooth sailing; CEO Bill Oplinger has warned that the new tariffs could cost up to 100,000 American jobs in the aluminum industry.

Potential Tariff Impacts

Alcoa Corporation is a global player involved in producing and selling bauxite, alumina, and aluminum products. The company primarily engages in bauxite mining operations and its conversion into alumina and other industrial chemical products.

President Donald Trump’s proposed 25% tariffs on goods from Mexico and Canada are raising concerns within the aluminum industry. According to Alcoa CEO Bill Oplinger, these tariffs could potentially result in the loss of 20,000 direct U.S. aluminum industry jobs and 80,000 indirect jobs. Oplinger further opined that the U.S. is already short of 4 million metric tons of aluminum annually, a deficit that is offset predominantly by imports from Mexico and Canada.

The tariffs have prompted Alcoa to advocate for an exemption on Canadian imports, which would allow approximately two-thirds of the metal consumed in the U.S. to continue being imported tariff-free. If a tariff is waged against Canada, it could shift global aluminum production to Europe.

Strong Recent Financial Growth and Outlook

In Q4 2024, Alcoa saw revenue rise by 20% to $3.5 billion, and net income increased by 124% to $202 million, or $0.76 per common share. For FY 2024, the revenue rose by 13% to $11.9 billion, and the net income grew to $60 million, or $0.26 per common share. The adjusted EBITDA, excluding special items, rose to $1.6 billion.

The company finished 2024 with a cash balance of $1.1 billion, reflecting proceeds of $737 million from a green bond issuance and repayment of Alumina Limited debt worth $385 million.

Management has given guidance to expect the total Alumina segment production in 2025 to range between 9.5 to 9.7 million metric tons and shipments between 13.1 and 13.3 million metric tons. The total Aluminum segment production is expected to range between 2.3 and 2.5 million metric tons, with shipments between 2.6 and 2.8 million metric tons.

Disclosure
data.author: Casey Dylan, CIMA
